doctor_turn: Hello, I’m Dr. Lee. What brought you in today?
patient_turn: Hi, um, I’ve been having this cough for like five days now, and it’s getting worse. It’s mostly during the day and at night, and I also have a sore throat and, um, I’ve been having trouble tasting food for the last couple of days. I’m kinda worried about it.

doctor_turn: When did the cough first start?
patient_turn: Uh, I think it started about five days ago. It just kinda came out of nowhere, and, um, it’s been getting worse since then.

doctor_turn: Has the cough changed in any way since it started?
patient_turn: Um, yeah, it’s definitely gotten worse. At first, it was just a little annoying, but now it’s, like, really persistent, you know? And I don’t have any sputum or anything, just a dry cough.

doctor_turn: Where do you feel the cough the most? Is it in your chest or throat?
patient_turn: I feel it mostly in my throat, um, like it’s really scratchy and uncomfortable. It’s kinda hard to swallow too, especially when I’m eating.

doctor_turn: What does the cough feel like? Is it a dry cough or does it feel different?
patient_turn: Um, I think it’s mostly in my throat, but I’m not really sure. It feels, like, kind of dry, I guess, but maybe not totally?

doctor_turn: Have you noticed any changes in your throat or taste recently?
patient_turn: Yeah, um, my throat has been really sore, especially when I swallow food. And, uh, I’ve also had trouble tasting things for the past couple of days, which is kinda weird and frustrating.

doctor_turn: Can you tell me where the cough feels the strongest?
How would you describe the cough—does it happen all day or mostly at certain times?
patient_turn: Oh, I actually don’t have a cough at all. I mean, I did, but now I think it’s gone. So, yeah, no cough right now.

doctor_turn: What other symptoms have you noticed along with the cough?
patient_turn: Um, besides the cough, I’ve had a sore throat and, like, trouble tasting food for the past couple of days. I’ve also felt a bit tired, but, um, nothing else really, like no fever or anything.

doctor_turn: Have you had any mucus or blood when you cough?
patient_turn: No, um, I haven’t had any mucus or blood when I cough. It’s just a dry cough, so, yeah, nothing like that.

doctor_turn: Have you experienced any nasal congestion or a loss of smell recently?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had any nasal congestion or loss of smell. It’s just been the cough and sore throat, you know?

doctor_turn: Have you had any night sweats or felt short of breath at all?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had any night sweats or felt short of breath. I mean, I felt a bit hot one night, but, um, I think that was just because I was worried or something.

doctor_turn: Have you ever had a cough or sore throat like this before?
patient_turn: Um, no, I haven’t had anything like this before. This is the first time I’ve experienced a cough and sore throat like this, so, honestly, I’m kinda worried.

doctor_turn: Have you had any past medical conditions or surgeries that I should know about?
patient_turn: No, I don’t have any past medical conditions or surgeries. My medical history is pretty unremarkable, I guess. The only thing is, I might have a penicillin allergy that causes a rash, but that’s about it.

doctor_turn: Does anyone in your family have health issues, like asthma or other conditions?
patient_turn: Um, yeah, my aunt has asthma, and my dad has Crohn’s disease. But, uh, that’s about it for my family history.

doctor_turn: Who do you live with at home?
patient_turn: I live with my parents and two younger siblings. It’s, um, a safe home environment, I guess.

doctor_turn: Do you smoke or drink alcohol regularly?
patient_turn: No, I don’t smoke or drink alcohol regularly. I’ve only had, like, a sip of wine once or twice, but that’s it.

doctor_turn: How do you attend school—online or in-person?
patient_turn: I attend school online, um, so I’ve been doing virtual classes. It’s been okay, but, you know, I’m kinda missing the in-person stuff.

doctor_turn: Are your vaccinations up to date?
patient_turn: Yeah, my vaccinations are up to date. I’ve gotten all the necessary ones, so, um, I’m good there.
